title: caltha palustris var. parnassifolia
intro: Caltha palustris var. parnassifolia, also known as Marsh Marigold or Parnassia, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Saxifragaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and glossy green leaves. The medicinal properties of Caltha palustris var. parnassifolia include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Caltha palustris var. parnassifolia can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, although its use should be under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner.
